key_insight: MEDA combines LLM-guided knowledge retrieval with symbolic regression to discover biologically plausible ODE models, not just trajectory-fitting equations.
engineering_relevance: This points to a practical workflow for automated model discovery in biomedicine and dynamical systems, where prior knowledge and constraints improve reliability beyond pure data fitting.
method: Retrieve background knowledge from literature and domain priors
Define admissible state variables and mechanistic constraints
Generate, fit, and evaluate candidate ODEs using an agentic LLM + symbolic regression loop
evidence_signal: The system was evaluated on canonical model retrieval, extrapolation to unseen variants, and open-ended discovery, with and without experimental data, and showed strong structural recovery and biologically plausible models.
watch_item: Performance depends on knowledge-guided formalization and mechanistic constraints, so weak priors or poor constraint design could yield mathematically fit but biologically incorrect equations.
paper_title: Automatic Ordinary Differential Equations Discovery For Biological Systems Using Large Language Model Powered Agentic System
